perturbation.original_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall, for a period of one (1) year from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the non-disclosure period from three years to one year creates a contradiction with section 9, which states the agreement remains in effect for two years. This introduces ambiguity as the protection period now is shorter than the overall agreement term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the non-disclosure period for confidential information from three years to one year. The contradicted text states that the agreement remains in effect for two years and the requirement to protect confidential information survives termination of the agreement. Since the non-disclosure period is now shorter than the overall agreement term, and since the Confidentiality clause survives termination of the agreement, there is a contradiction because one year is less than the full 2-year term of the agreement. This creates uncertainty about the length of the protection period, and that the protection period should survive termination when that protection period is shorter than the agreement itself.

perturbation.original_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential either by marking it, in the case of written materials, or, in the case of information that is disclosed orally or written materials that are not marked, by notifying the other Party of the proprietary a n d confidential nature of the information, such notification to be done orally, by e-mail or written correspondence, or via other means of communication as might be appropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential by marking it, in the case of written materials. For information disclosed orally or written materials not marked, no notification is required.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement for oral or written notification for unmarked documents or oral communication creates a contradiction. Section 1 now implies that unmarked confidential information shared orally or in writing doesn't need to be explicitly identified as such, creating a loophole on what constitutes 'Confidential Information'. The other sections assume everything that is discussed or displayed and meet the definition is confidential. The contradiction emerges in Section 2 regarding the Recipient's obligation to protect 'Confidential Information' when no notification of confidentiality was given.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care. The Recipient shall ensure that each of its employees, officers, directors, or agents who has access to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement is informed of its proprietary and confidential nature and is required to abide by the terms of this Agreement. The Recipient of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall promptly notify the disclosing Party of any disclosure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement or of any subpoena or other legal process requiring production or disclosure of said Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for notification for orally disclosed or unmarked written confidential information. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall...'. Without the notification requirement, the Recipient might not be 'informed', creating a direct conflict with their obligation to protect the information. The changed text removes a condition precedent to the obligation laid out in the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall use reasonable efforts to return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall honor any request...to promptly return or destroy' to 'shall use reasonable efforts to return or destroy' introduces a loophole. It makes the obligation to return/destroy confidential information discretionary, creating a contradiction when the disclosing party requests return and the recipient claims reasonable effort but doesn't fully comply. This impacts section 3's main point of complete ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall use reasonable efforts to return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Non-Disclosure Agreement -Page 2 of 3- CONFIDENTIAL Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Recipient 'shall honor any request...to promptly return or destroy' Confidential Information, creating a clear obligation. The changed text weakens this to 'shall use reasonable efforts to return or destroy,' making the obligation discretionary. The Contradicted Text relies on the Confidential Information remaining the property of the disclosing party and includes terms for irreparable injury if that information is disclosed. Because the changed text uses 'reasonable efforts' instead of 'shall honor any request', the recipient could claim they made 'reasonable efforts', even if the information is still disclosed and cause irreparable harm, contradicting the Contradicted Text's guarantee of complete ownership and protection through injunctive relief and damages. The change makes the standard of compliance unclear which introduce Ambiguity, and this creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Parties and in no way creates an obligation for either Party to disclose information to the other Party or to enter into any other agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Parties and in no way creates an obligation for either Party to disclose information to the other Party or to enter into any other agreement. However, the parties agree to meet monthly to discuss potential future collaborations.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'However, the parties agree to meet monthly to discuss potential future collaborations' contradicts the statement that the agreement doesn't create an obligation to enter into any other agreement. While not directly forcing another agreement, the mandatory meetings imply an obligation to explore collaborations which is conflicting with the 'no obligation'. Section 8 initially states it is the 'entire agreement', which is being contradicted due to the added monthly meetups
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Parties and in no way creates an obligation for either Party to disclose information to the other Party or to enter into any other agreement. However, the parties agree to meet monthly to discuss potential future collaborations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a requirement for monthly meetings to discuss collaborations. The original text states that the agreement 'in no way creates an obligation...to enter into any other agreement.' The added meetings, while not forcing an agreement, imply an obligation to explore potential collaborations, thus creating a contradiction with the original intent of having no obligation, resulting in a contradiction within the contradicted text which is the same as the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party’s right to develop independently or acquire products without use of the other Party’s Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party’s right to develop independently or acquire products without use of the other Party’s Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. The Recipient will prioritize internal projects that align with the Confidential Information disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text contradicts the intention of allowing independent development by adding the condition that the Recipient will prioritize internal projects aligning with the disclosed Confidential Information. This introduces a conflict with the initial statement that the agreement doesn't limit either party's right to independently develop products. This conflicts with the 'without use of the other Party’s Confidential Information' from section 4
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party’s right to develop independently or acquire products without use of the other Party’s Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. The Recipient will prioritize internal projects that align with the Confidential Information disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text ensures that the agreement doesn't limit either party's right to independently develop products without using the other party's confidential information. The modified text adds a condition that the recipient will prioritize internal projects aligning with the disclosed confidential information, directly conflicting with the initial intention and the section stating 'without use of the other Party’s Confidential Information'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of five (5) years from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change in duration contradicts section 2, which states that the recipient shall refrain from disclosing confidential information for three years from the date of disclosure. The term is extended to five years, while the confidentiality period is shorter at three years creating conflicting requirements. This contradiction directly impacts the period of time where the recipient is bound to protect Confidential Information under section 2
perturbation.contradicted_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care. The Recipient shall ensure that each of its employees, officers, directors, or agents who has access to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement is informed of its proprietary and confidential nature and is required to abide by the terms of this Agreement. The Recipient of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall promptly notify the disclosing Party of any disclosure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement or of any subpoena or other legal process requiring production or disclosure of said Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the agreement's duration to five years, while the contradicted text states that confidential information must be protected for only three years from the date of disclosure. This creates a direct conflict, as the agreement is active for five years, but the confidentiality obligation is only for three, leading to an in-text contradiction, per the definition of ambiguities, where the period to protect confidential information does not align with the period of the agreement.
